title: Where Property Becomes Forest

features.name: fence-dissolving
features.desc-on-touch: The boundary oxidized into rust and then into root. Trees pushed through the metal. The question of passage or barrier became irrelevant. The forest decided it was one place.

features.name: reclaimed-marker
features.desc-on-touch: A property marker stone, split by roots. The numbers worn away. The land has reclaimed its own naming.

features.name: overgrowth-path
features.desc-on-touch: A trail worn not by people but by the forest moving itself. Trees reroute. Understory learns to walk.

inscription: the boundary between owned and wild learned to dissolve; now the archive doesn't know if the trees were always here or are still arriving

file.name: Property_Deed_Survey_Year_1994_Lost_to_Undergrowth.txt
file.note: The last record before the forest decided there were no more boundaries. The archive filed it but the location keeps moving.

exits.label: back toward the scrub's interior, where boundaries have become suggestions
deeper into forest, where property lines dissolved into root networks
exits.to: NEW: the inherited grove where ownership became symbiosis

exits.label: outward where the first trees remember when this was cleared
